name: Oyster Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk and cream in a large heavy saucepan over moderately high heat.
2. Drain the oyster liquor into a separate saucepan and bring to a boil.
3. In a third saucepan, spoon 2 tablespoons of the hot liquor, add the oysters and butter, and heat uncovered over moderate heat, swirling the oysters around for 3 to 4 minutes until their edges just begin to ruffle.
4. Add the oysters at once to the hot milk mixture, mix in the hot oyster liquor, salt, celery salt, pepper, and paprika.
5. Ladle into heated bowls and serve piping hot with oyster crackers.
This is based on the oyster stew at New York's famous Grand Central Oyster Bar.
